tools.name: verify_object
tools.description: Re-derive a UOR object's did from its content and report whether it self-verifies (Law L5).

tools.name: resolve_object
tools.description: Resolve a did:holo / holo:// resource to its self-verifying UOR object.

tools.name: holo_describe
tools.description: The STANDARDIZED, application-agnostic capability card for THIS holospace — present identically on every Holo app's MCP server. One call returns a self-verifying W3C JSON-LD object (schema.org SoftwareApplication + a schema:Action per tool with its input schema + published resources + conformsTo + MCP protocol metadata): who the app is, what it can do, how to invoke it. Re-derive the card's id to verify it (Law L5). The uniform entry point for an agent to introspect ANY holospace. No args (also published as the resource holo://capabilities).

tools.name: ask_model
tools.description: Ask the connected agent's OWN model a question (MCP sampling — the INVERSE direction: a holospace borrows the agent's intelligence). Requires a sampling-capable client.

tools.name: ask_user
tools.description: Ask the human, via the agent host, for input (MCP elicitation). Requires an elicitation-capable client.

tools.name: list_roots
tools.description: List the file:// roots the agent host has granted access to (MCP roots). Requires a roots-capable client.

tools.name: verify_batch
tools.description: Verify many UOR objects at once; reports progress and is cancellable.

tools.name: holo_rank
tools.description: Personal, content-addressed PageRank (HoloRank). Given reference edges {rel,from,to,by?,weight?} and a seed (teleport set of did:holo you trust/own), returns a SELF-VERIFYING UOR ranking object. The result is deterministic, so DON'T trust it — re-derive its did (verify_object, Law L5) and/or recompute holo_rank over the same edges to confirm the same κ. Trustless ranking for agents.

tools.name: holo_recommend
tools.description: One round-trip personal recommendations. Give the apps/did:holo you use ('usage'); the server fetches only YOUR neighbourhood by content address (bounded — taste-aligned reviewers + their picks, not the whole corpus) and ranks it, returning recs (each with a reason) plus a SELF-VERIFYING UOR result you re-derive (Law L5) and can recompute (deterministic). Requires the server to be wired to a review corpus.

usage.description: app ids / did:holo you already use

tools.name: render_qml
tools.description: Parse + EXECUTE a QML document with Holo QML (the from-spec engine that runs the real upstream SDDM greeter in the browser — Qt 6 QML Reference semantics, no Qt runtime) and return its live component tree + signal wiring as a SELF-VERIFYING UOR object: re-derive its did (verify_object, Law L5); deterministic, so an agent recomputes the same κ. Lets an agent introspect any QML UI's structure + the API it wires, without a browser. Input: { qml } (the QML source — e.g. resolve the greeter's source via resolve_object first). The display manager's own greeter is also published as the resource usr/share/sddm/greeter.uor.json.

tools.name: holo_build
tools.description: BUILD a holospace app: compile source (Holo-C — a C subset: int functions, arithmetic, if/while, short-circuit logic, calls, mutual recursion) to spec-valid WebAssembly, persisted by its content address (κ). Deterministic + O(1) on repeat (identical source rebinds, not recompiles). Returns { ok, kappa (the artifact did:holo), sourceKappa, receipt (a self-verifying PROV-O build receipt κ), exports }. On a compile error returns { ok:false, error:{ message, line, col } } — FIX the source at that line and call holo_build again (the tight build loop). COMPOSE by content address: declare `extern int f(int a) from "did:holo:…";` (or `str` params/results for strings) to import another component's export BY ITS κ — the response's `imports` lists the dependency κ's, holo_run links them automatically. Serverless, self-verifying (Law L5).

tools.name: holo_run
tools.description: RUN a built app by content address. Pass { kappa } (an ARTIFACT κ runs directly; a SOURCE κ self-compiles then runs) OR { source } to build-then-run. Optionally { fn, args } to call an exported function with i32 args and get its result. Returns { ok, kappa, selfCompiled, exports:[names], result? }. The wasm is re-derived before running (Law L5). No server.

tools.name: holo_share
tools.description: SHARE a built app: the κ IS the share. Returns { kappa, holo (holo://κ), url } — location-independent, self-verifying, self-compiling; the recipient resolves it from cache/peers/IPFS/origin, re-derives it (Law L5), and runs it, with no server. Agents collaborate by passing κ.

kappa.description: the artifact or source κ to share

tools.name: holo_compile_model
tools.description: COMPILE a neural-network model into a content-addressed .holo κ-object. Pass an ONNX model as { onnxBase64 } (or { url } to fetch one); the REAL hologram-ai compiler (import → optimize → lower → compile, ADR-0017) runs in WebAssembly — in the browser or Node, no server, no toolchain. Compiling is a κ-transform (κ(onnx) ⊕ κ(compiler) → κ(holo)). Returns { ok, kappa (the compiled model's did:holo), sourceKappa (the ONNX κ), compilerKappa, bytes, ports ({inputs,outputs} each with dtype + shape), holoBase64 (the archive bytes — re-derive: sha256(decode)==kappa, Law L5), receipt (a self-verifying PROV-O compile receipt κ) }. Turns ANY ONNX model into a substrate-native, shareable, re-derivable object — the general model front-end that broadens the catalog beyond the hand-built ternary engine. Serverless (Law L1/L5).

url.description: or a URL to fetch the ONNX model from (ingest boundary)

tools.name: holo_inspect
tools.description: INSPECT a UOR object — the agent side of the on-screen Inspect (identical to what a human sees right-clicking an object). Pass the object's bytes as `source`; returns { kappa (re-derived did:holo), type (bundle·module·svg·json·text), bytes, children (the composition DAG — child κ's, for a bundle), exports (for a module) }. Self-verifying: the κ is derived from the bytes you passed (Law L5). Use it to understand an object before remixing it.

source.description: the object's bytes as text (e.g. from holo_resolve)

tools.name: holo_remix
tools.description: REMIX an object → a NEW self-verifying object (the agent side of on-screen Edit; identical to a human's). Pass the edited `source` (optionally the `parent` κ it was forked from). Returns { kappa (new did:holo = sha256 of the bytes), holo (holo://κ), parent, link }. `link` is a SELF-CONTAINED, cross-device, serverless share URL (`/apps/ui/render.html#k=<κ>&o=<gzipped bytes>`): the recipient — human in a browser, or another agent — decodes it, re-derives the κ (Law L5), and renders it on ANY device with no server. An edit is a FORK (a new κ), never a mutation (Law L1). The remix loop for agents: holo_resolve → holo_inspect → holo_remix → hand the link to a human/agent.

source.description: the edited object bytes as text

parent.description: optional κ this was forked from

tools.name: own_verify
tools.description: Verify a content-addressed OWNERSHIP chain (ADR-053 Titles): re-derive every Title κ, check each transfer's signature + authority (the current owner, or an attenuated UCAN delegation) + lineage (Law L5 / SEC-2), and report who owns it NOW. Returns { ok, owner (σ-axis κ), ownerDid (did:holo), head, errors, result } — `result` is a SELF-VERIFYING UOR object (re-derive its id, Law L5). An agent verifies ownership without trusting any server. An agent ACQUIRES ownership by signing a Title locally (its own holo-identity) and verifying the extended chain here.

delegations.description: optional { titleκ: delegation } proofs for delegated transfers

tools.name: own_settle
tools.description: Settle value against a PROVEN Title (ADR-053/048): releases a voucher ONLY if the ownership chain re-derives AND the payer's order commits to the proven head — pay against proven ownership, not claimed. Keyless + anyone-runs; a tampered/unproven Title releases nothing. Returns { released, voucher } (voucher κ = the idempotent txId).

tools.name: own_passport
tools.description: An ownership passport for an object: verify its Title chain and summarise { owner, ownerDid, verified, history (number of transfers), head } as a SELF-VERIFYING UOR object (re-derive its id, Law L5). The agent-facing 'who owns this, provably' tool.

tools.name: bittensor_snapshot
tools.description: Project a Bittensor subnet's metagraph at a block into the substrate's agent fabric (ADR-0071): returns a κ-rooted dcat:Catalog of neuron AgentFacts whose root did:holo PINS the block hash — a self-verifying UOR object you re-derive (verify_object, Law L5). A Bittensor subnet IS a NANDA registry. Reading the chain is an INGEST BOUNDARY (Subtensor public JSON-RPC); omit args for the deterministic sample subnet. Returns { root, catalog, netuid, block, blockHash, neurons:[{hotkey,did,factsId,consensus,stake}], records }.

snapshot.description: optional { netuid, block, blockHash, neurons:[…] }; omit for the sample subnet

tools.name: bittensor_agentfacts
tools.description: Project ONE Bittensor neuron to a dual-trust AgentFacts document (ADR-0071/034): on the same bytes a valid NANDA AgentFacts record · a self-verifying UOR object (re-derive its id) · a W3C VC signed by the neuron's hotkey · a did:pkh chain principal. Returns { agentFacts (self-verifying), did, dualTrust (the hotkey signature verifies) }.

hotkey.description: the neuron's ss58 hotkey (defaults to the sample subnet's first neuron)

tools.name: bittensor_infer
tools.description: Query a Bittensor miner and seal the answer as a re-derivable PROV-O inference receipt (ADR-0071, Holo Q idiom ADR-0052) bound to the neuron/subnet/block + signed by the hotkey. A stochastic answer is RECEIPT-verifiable (re-derive its id; the response κ binds the text), not reproducible. Returns { answer, receipt (self-verifying), verified }.

hotkey.description: which neuron (defaults to the sample subnet's validator)

tools.name: bittensor_settle
tools.description: Pay TAO ONLY against PROVEN work (ADR-0071, Orchestrate ADR-045 + Settle ADR-048): composes the inference receipts into a PROV-O work-receipt DAG and releases a TAO voucher per step only if the work re-derives AND the conscience gate accepts — tampered work pays nothing. TESTNET-GATED. Returns { workReceipt, released:[voucher…], withheld, network, verified }.

prompts.description: the questions to settle (defaults to two sample prompts)

tools.name: wallet_get_address
tools.description: Holo Wallet (Tether-WDK parity) — read-only. Return the wallet's address for a chain (ethereum, base, arbitrum, optimism, polygon, bsc, avalanche, gnosis, linea, scroll, celo, blast, plasma, bitcoin, solana, hyperliquid). No value moves; no signature. Routed through the human-gated wallet seam (holo-wallet-bridge); keys never leave the wallet. An AI agent must present a wallet:read delegation; Q must hold the user's standing read-consent or ask. See holo-wallet-agent.mjs for the full 7-category surface and its self-verifying capability card.

tools.name: wallet_get_balance
tools.description: Holo Wallet — read-only. Native-asset balance for a chain from live RPC (failover, no custodian). Value never moves. Agent needs wallet:read; Q asks unless granted standing read-consent. Default-deny otherwise.

tools.name: wallet_get_token_balance
tools.description: Holo Wallet — read-only. ERC-20 (EVM) / SPL (Solana) token balance from live RPC. e.g. USD₮ across chains. Agent needs wallet:read; Q asks unless granted standing read-consent.

tools.name: pricing_get_price
tools.description: Holo Wallet — read-only. USD spot price per asset (public price feed). Value never moves.

tools.name: indexer_get_history
tools.description: Holo Wallet — read-only. Recent transaction history for the wallet's account on a chain (public explorer/RPC). Value never moves.

tools.name: wallet_sign_message
tools.description: Holo Wallet — SIGN (key-touching). Sign a plain message (EIP-191) with the wallet's key. The wallet — not the caller — produces the signature, ONLY after a fresh payload-bound biometric approval (default-deny). An AI agent must present a wallet:sign delegation; Q must obtain explicit per-action user approval (never standing). The key never leaves the wallet.

tools.name: wallet_sign_typed_data
tools.description: Holo Wallet — SIGN (key-touching). Sign EIP-712 typed data (e.g. a dapp/exchange order). Human biometric-gated, default-deny; agent needs wallet:spend; Q needs explicit per-action approval. Key never leaves the wallet.

tools.name: wallet_send
tools.description: Holo Wallet — SPEND (moves value, IRREVERSIBLE). Send native or token value on a chain; broadcasts to the live network. The wallet signs ONLY after a fresh payload-bound biometric ceremony over THIS exact recipient+amount (default-deny, fail-closed). An AI agent must present a wallet:spend delegation that itself holds that capability (authority can only attenuate, SEC-2); Q must obtain explicit per-action user approval and may NEVER hold a standing spend grant. Re-derives a κ receipt (Law L5).

token.description: optional token contract/mint; omit for native

tools.name: swap_execute
tools.description: Holo Wallet — SPEND. Execute a spot swap: Solana via Jupiter (inputMint/outputMint) OR EVM via Velora/ParaSwap (set chain + srcToken/destToken; native sentinel 0xEeee…). The route is untrusted: the wallet re-derives a min-out floor, asserts the SEALED router/program, simulates (eth_call / Solana sim) — THEN gates the human, THEN signs. Agent needs wallet:spend; Q needs explicit per-action approval. Key never leaves the wallet.

chain.description: omit/solana = Jupiter; an EVM chain = Velora

tools.name: bridge_get_quote
tools.description: Holo Wallet — read-only. Quote a USD₮0 cross-chain bridge over LayerZero OFT: the native messaging fee + minimum received on the destination. Value never moves. srcChain/dstChain are chain names (ethereum, arbitrum, optimism, polygon, plasma, hyperliquid); the destination endpoint (dstEid) is re-derived from a pinned table, never trusted from input.

tools.name: bridge_execute
tools.description: Holo Wallet — SPEND. Bridge USD₮0 across chains via LayerZero OFT. The destination (dstEid) is re-derived from a pinned EID table, the recipient is your address→bytes32, the SEALED OFT contract is asserted (a drainer `to` is refused), the LZ fee is quoted, and the send() tx is simulated — THEN the human gate, THEN sign (value = the native messaging fee). Agent needs wallet:spend; Q needs explicit per-action approval. Key never leaves the wallet.

tools.name: lending_positions
tools.description: Holo Wallet — read-only. Read the wallet's Aave V3 position on a chain: total collateral, total debt, available-to-borrow (all USD, 8dp), liquidation threshold, LTV, and HEALTH FACTOR. Value never moves. Re-derived live from the chain (getUserAccountData on the sealed Pool).

chain.description: ethereum, arbitrum, optimism, polygon, avalanche, gnosis

tools.name: lending_supply
tools.description: Holo Wallet — SPEND. Supply an asset as collateral to Aave V3. The SEALED Pool is asserted (a drainer is refused), allowance checked, and the tx eth_call-simulated — THEN the human gate, THEN sign. Agent needs wallet:spend; Q needs explicit per-action approval. Key never leaves the wallet.

tools.name: lending_borrow
tools.description: Holo Wallet — SPEND. Borrow an asset against Aave V3 collateral (variable rate). Borrow capacity is re-derived and the tx simulated (Aave reverts an unsafe borrow that would risk liquidation) BEFORE the human gate. Agent needs wallet:spend; Q needs explicit per-action approval. Key never leaves the wallet.

tools.name: fiat_get_quote
tools.description: Holo Wallet — read-only. Quote a MoonPay fiat on-ramp purchase (how much crypto a fiat amount buys, incl. fees). Needs the operator's MoonPay publishable key configured. Value never moves.

tools.name: fiat_buy
tools.description: Holo Wallet — SPEND-class consent. Buy crypto with fiat via MoonPay. An on-ramp DEPOSITS to the wallet, so there is NO key signature; instead the wallet builds a widget URL bound to the SEALED MoonPay origin and YOUR re-derived address (funds cannot be redirected elsewhere) and the human gate consents before it opens. Completion is verified by the on-chain deposit (ingest boundary), not MoonPay's word. Agent needs wallet:spend; Q needs explicit per-action approval.

tools.name: aa_account_address
tools.description: Holo Wallet — read-only. Return the wallet's counterfactual ERC-4337 smart-account address (SimpleAccount v0.7), re-derived from the SEALED factory on an EVM chain. Value never moves.

tools.name: aa_send
tools.description: Holo Wallet — SPEND. Build + sign an ERC-4337 UserOperation (execute to→value→data) from the smart account. The SEALED EntryPoint is asserted and the userOpHash re-derived BEFORE the human gate; the EOA key signs only after consent. Returns the signed UserOp for a bundler to submit (submission out-of-band). Agent needs wallet:spend; Q needs per-action approval. Key never leaves the wallet.

tools.name: aa_authorize_7702
tools.description: Holo Wallet — SPEND. Sign an EIP-7702 authorization delegating this EOA to a smart-contract implementation (the 'gasless' account upgrade). The authHash is re-derived and the signature self-checked to recover to the owner BEFORE return; the type-4 tx is relayer/sponsor-submitted (out-of-band). Agent needs wallet:spend; Q needs per-action approval.

tools.name: x402_pay
tools.description: Holo Wallet — SPEND. Pay an x402 HTTP 402 challenge: sign an EIP-3009 USD₮ `transferWithAuthorization` (gasless, off-chain) authorizing the seller to pull the exact amount. A payment IS an EIP-712 signature, so it rides the SAME human-gated signTypedData door — default-deny, payload-bound biometric, key never leaves the wallet. An AI agent must present a wallet:spend delegation (authority only attenuates, SEC-2); Q must obtain explicit per-action approval (never standing). The facilitator verifies by re-deriving the digest and recovering the signer (fail-closed on tamper/underpay/expiry/replay) before settling on-chain. Input: the 402 `requirements` (from makeRequirements), the payer `from` address, and optional chain/validBefore/nonce. Returns { xPayment (base64 X-PAYMENT header), payload }.

prompts.name: verify_object
prompts.description: Verify a UOR object by re-deriving its did (Law L5).

prompts.name: conformance_brief
prompts.description: Explain Hologram OS's content-addressed, self-verifying model to an agent.

note: Static discovery document. The aggregate endpoint is /mcp; every app is ALSO its own MCP server at /~<app>/mcp (see `servers`). Run `npx hologram-mcp` (stdio) or `--http` for a live endpoint; resources are self-verifying UOR objects.
